briefing.headline: Pitch it tight — winter muddy water puts a jig in its best element.
briefing.gameplan:
In winter with muddy water and stable conditions, jigs presentations shine.

Johnston: Use a compact mini flipping jig instead of full-size. You catch more fish and get more bites. With ever-increasing fishing pressure, a smaller jig is what it takes to get bit.

Key adjustment for stable: moderate cadence — match the natural pace of local forage.

primaryApproach.retrieve: Johnston: Use a compact mini flipping jig instead of full-size. You catch more fish and get more bites. With ever-increasing fishing pressure, a smaller jig is what it takes to get bit.
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on grass, flat, creek channel. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: Drop the bait to about 2 ft above the fish's head — smallmouth were feeding up, so dropping it in front of them on the bottom meant they wouldn't bite. Keep bait above their heads and they co
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on grass, flat, creek channel. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: KVD: 'Winter bass move shallower than most realize — if you can reach them with crankbait, jerkbait, or slow-rolled spinnerbait in 10ft or less, power baits are first option. Fish avoid expansive flats unless warming trend pushes them to gravel po...

Wheeler: Big fish spots are big giant flats in 22-29 ft with tiny isolated rockpiles — a boulder in the middle of a rocky flat, or a hard-bottom spot. Only good for 1-2 fish and time-consuming to find, but that's what separates you

Hackney: 'I let water temperature dictate which jig to throw in shallow cover. Warm water means swim jig, cold water means flipping jig. It only takes a few casts to determine which method works better'

briefing.depthStrategy: Stable winter conditions position bass at 15-35ft on main lake structure — bluffs, steep points, channel bends. Fish are lethargic and grouped tight. Vertical presentations directly on structure are essential.
adjustIf: If conditions suddenly shift (pressure drop, wind change), be ready to speed up your approach — transitional moments produce big bites
If you find a cleaner pocket or tributary, switch to natural presentations — fish in cleaner water see more lures
If bites are subtle, try a lighter jig weight — slower fall rate can be the difference
